---
title: "Southern Beeblossom (Oenothera simulans)"
slug: "oenothera-simulans"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474725102/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Southern Beeblossom"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Myrtales"
tax_family: "Onagraceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Myrtales", "Onagraceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474725102/medium.jpg" alt="Southern Beeblossom" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/scarnevale">
scarnevale</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264274864">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Myrtales
-   **Family:** Onagraceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=914039)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not available
-   **Rarity note:** Common across Florida.

## Natural history notes

A slender wildflower characterized by its narrow leaves and delicate white to pale pink flowers that open in the evening. In Florida, it is found in sandy, open habitats such as scrub, sandhills, and disturbed areas. It typically blooms during the warmer months and is pollinated by nocturnal insects such as moths.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/914039-Oenothera-simulans)
-   [Local profile](https://www.flawildflowers.org/flower-friday-oenothera-simulans/)
